articles.title: Homeowner’s Guide to Yard Drainage in Central Texas
articles.excerpt: Learn how to spot drainage issues in your yard, what they mean for your foundation, and which fixes actually work in Central Texas soils.
articles.content: Standing water near your foundation or along fence lines is more than a nuisance—it can lead to structural movement, wood rot, and erosion. In this guide we walk through common yard drainage problems in Central Texas, why clay soils make them worse, and what you can do about it. We cover grading basics, French drains, surface drains, and when to call a civil engineer versus a contractor. You’ll also learn what survey data helps us design long-term solutions that keep water moving away from your home.

articles.title: Stormwater Management Basics for Small Commercial Sites
articles.excerpt: A practical overview of detention, water quality, and conveyance requirements for small commercial projects under five acres.
articles.content: Even small developments can trigger stormwater requirements under local ordinances and state regulations. This article explains how runoff is calculated, what detention and water-quality controls are, and how early coordination between your civil engineer, architect, and surveyor can prevent redesign late in permitting. We include example site layouts, common review comments from Central Texas municipalities, and tips to keep construction costs under control.

articles.title: Understanding Surface Drainage Around Your Home
articles.excerpt: An in-depth look at how surface drainage should work around slabs, crawlspaces, and basements.
articles.content: Proper surface drainage starts with lot grading plans and is verified in the field with as-built surveys. In this article we explain slope standards, swales, and how curb inlets interact with private drainage patterns...

articles.title: What Is a Boundary Survey and When Do You Need One?
articles.excerpt: Boundary surveys clarify where your property begins and ends, protecting you from costly disputes.
articles.content: A boundary survey identifies the legally described limits of your property using a combination of deed research, monument recovery, and precise field measurements...

articles.title: Why a Good Topographic Survey Makes Design Cheaper
articles.excerpt: Topographic data is the foundation of every efficient grading and drainage plan.
articles.content: Civil engineers rely on accurate elevations and existing-feature data to design efficient sites...

articles.title: Site Plans for Residential Building Permits: What Cities Look For
articles.excerpt: If your city asked for a site plan, here’s what that usually means and which details are critical for review.
articles.content: Most Central Texas cities require a scaled site plan drawn from current survey data when issuing permits for new homes, additions, and pools...

articles.title: Announcing Our New Dallas Civil Engineering Office
articles.excerpt: We’ve expanded our civil engineering team with a new Dallas office focused on land development and transportation.
articles.content: To better serve clients in North Texas, we’ve opened a Dallas office offering full-service civil engineering and surveying...

articles.title: Complete Streets Concepts for Suburban Arterials
articles.excerpt: How to balance vehicle capacity with safety, bikes, and pedestrians on growing suburban corridors.
articles.content: Suburban arterials in Texas are carrying more traffic than they were ever intended to handle...

articles.title: 5 Common Drainage Myths That Cost Homeowners Money
articles.excerpt: Not every wet spot needs a French drain. We break down which fixes work—and which waste money.
articles.content: From overusing French drains to ignoring grading, there are several persistent myths about residential drainage...

articles.title: Low-Cost Stormwater Retrofits for Existing Parking Lots
articles.excerpt: Add bioretention, curb cuts, and other simple measures to improve stormwater performance without full reconstruction.
articles.content: Existing parking lots often lack modern stormwater controls. We explore retrofit strategies like bioretention islands, permeable bands, and curb inlets that can be added during restriping or minor rehab...

articles.title: How FEMA Floodplains Affect Your Residential Project
articles.excerpt: Before you build near a creek, understand how FEMA maps and local regulations impact your plans.
articles.content: Building in or near FEMA floodplains requires additional study, permits, and often an elevation certificate...

articles.title: Best Practices for Residential Site Grading in Expansive Clay Soils
articles.excerpt: Expansive clays make grading design in Central Texas uniquely challenging. Here’s how we address it.
articles.content: Expansive clay soils shrink and swell with moisture changes, which can wreak havoc on foundations and pavements...

job_postings.title: Civil Engineer I - Land Development (Dallas)
job_postings.description: Our Dallas office is seeking an entry-level Civil Engineer to support site development projects including residential subdivisions, commercial pads, and public infrastructure. This role works closely with senior engineers on grading, drainage, utility, and paving plans.
job_postings.requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or related field; EIT preferred or ability to obtain within 12 months; strong AutoCAD Civil 3D skills; interest in land development; 0–2 years of relevant experience.

job_postings.title: Civil Engineer II - Commercial & Mixed-Use (Dallas)
job_postings.description: The Civil Engineer II will lead design tasks for commercial and mixed-use developments in the Dallas–Fort Worth area, including site layout, grading, drainage, and utility design.
job_postings.requirements: BSCE; PE or ability to obtain within 12 months; 3–7 years of experience in site development; proficiency in Civil 3D and stormwater modeling software.

job_postings.title: Survey Crew Chief (Austin)
job_postings.description: Lead a two- to three-person field crew on boundary, topographic, construction staking, and ALTA/NSPS surveys in the Greater Austin area.
job_postings.requirements: 5+ years of field survey experience; prior crew chief responsibility; strong knowledge of GPS and robotic total stations; valid driver’s license.

job_postings.title: Survey Technician I (Round Rock)
job_postings.description: Entry-level office survey technician assisting with drafting boundary, topographic, and subdivision plats under the supervision of an RPLS.
job_postings.requirements: Certificate or associate degree in surveying or drafting preferred; working knowledge of AutoCAD; strong attention to detail.

job_postings.title: Civil Engineering Intern (Summer - Austin)
job_postings.description: This summer internship provides exposure to land development and municipal engineering projects. Interns will assist with drafting, calculations, and field observations.
job_postings.requirements: Current junior or senior in an accredited civil engineering program; basic CAD skills; strong communication and willingness to learn.

job_postings.title: Senior Civil Engineer / Project Manager - Roadway (San Antonio)
job_postings.description: Lead roadway and transportation projects including arterials, intersections, and corridor studies for public-sector clients.
job_postings.requirements: BSCE; Texas PE; 8+ years of roadway design experience; proven project management and client interaction skills.

job_postings.title: Operations Coordinator
job_postings.description: Coordinate scheduling, resource allocation, and project setup for our surveying and engineering teams in Austin.
job_postings.requirements: 3–5 years of experience in a professional services environment; strong organizational skills; familiarity with project management software.

job_postings.title: Front Desk Administrator (Round Rock)
job_postings.description: Serve as the first point of contact for clients and visitors, manage phones, and support basic administrative tasks.
job_postings.requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; strong communication and customer service skills; proficiency with Microsoft Office.

resources.title: Homeowner Boundary Survey Guide for Central Texas
resources.description: A plain-language PDF guide explaining what a boundary survey is, when you need one for a home purchase, and how lots under 1 acre are typically handled in Austin and surrounding cities.

resources.title: Understanding FEMA Elevation Certificates for Homeowners
resources.description: Step-by-step explanation of elevation certificates, why your lender or insurer may require one, and how they relate to FEMA flood maps.

resources.title: Residential Site Plan Checklist
resources.description: A printable checklist outlining the typical information cities require on a residential site plan, including setbacks, easements, and drainage arrows.

resources.title: Drainage and Grading Basics for Homeowners
resources.description: An introductory guide to understanding how grading and simple drainage improvements can protect your home.

resources.title: Owner’s Guide to ALTA/NSPS Land Title Surveys
resources.description: Explains Table A items, typical schedules, and how an ALTA/NSPS survey fits into commercial real estate due diligence.

resources.title: Topographic Survey Technical Specification Template
resources.description: A technical template spec for architects and engineers to include in RFPs and project manuals.

resources.title: Homebuyer Survey Options Comparison Sheet
resources.description: One-page comparison of boundary, location, and topographic surveys for residential buyers.

resources.title: Sample Completed Elevation Certificate
resources.description: An anonymized example of a completed FEMA Elevation Certificate with annotations explaining each section.

resources.title: Commercial Project Kickoff Checklist
resources.description: Checklist for developers and owners to organize surveying, civil engineering, and geotechnical tasks at project start.

resources.title: Floodplain & Drainage FAQ
resources.description: Web-based FAQ answering common questions about floodplains, drainage easements, and elevation certificates.

services.name: Residential Boundary Survey – Sub-1-Acre Lots (Austin)
services.description_short: Boundary survey for single-family residential lots under 1 acre within the Austin city limits and nearby suburbs.
services.description_long: This service is tailored for homeowners, buyers, and real estate professionals working with single-family residential lots under 1 acre in Austin, Round Rock, Pflugerville, and surrounding areas. Our field crews recover existing monuments where available, set new markers as needed, and prepare a signed and sealed boundary survey drawing suitable for title companies, lenders, and permit applications. Pricing assumes typical access and record conditions; lots with extensive vegetation, difficult access, or complex deed histories may require additional effort.

services.name: Topographic Survey – Commercial Sites
services.description_short: Detailed topographic and existing-conditions survey for small to mid-size commercial developments.
services.description_long: Our commercial topographic survey provides 1-foot (or finer) contour intervals, spot elevations, and mapping of visible improvements, utilities, and drainage features. This level of detail is suitable for site design, grading plans, stormwater analysis, and architectural coordination. The listed starting price applies to relatively open sites up to 2 acres; larger or more complex properties will be quoted individually.

services.name: ALTA/NSPS Land Title Survey
services.description_short: Comprehensive ALTA/NSPS Land Title Survey for commercial real estate transactions and financing.
services.description_long: Our ALTA/NSPS Land Title Surveys are prepared in accordance with the latest ALTA/NSPS standards, including selected Table A options. We coordinate closely with your title company and legal team to resolve boundary, easement, and encroachment issues prior to closing. The starting price shown assumes a relatively simple urban parcel under 2 acres with a current title commitment. Complex easement structures, multiple parcels, or tight closing schedules may increase fees.

services.name: Residential Site Plan
services.description_short: Engineer-prepared residential site plan for building permits, including grading and basic drainage for lots up to 1 acre.
services.description_long: This service provides a signed and sealed site plan suitable for building permit submittal for new homes, additions, and accessory structures on lots up to approximately 1 acre. Using current survey data, we prepare a scaled plan showing building footprint, driveways, sidewalks, setbacks, easements, and proposed grading. Basic drainage arrows and spot elevations are included to demonstrate positive drainage away from the structure. Optional add-ons include detailed drainage design or retaining wall plans.

services.name: Residential Boundary + Topographic Survey Combo
services.description_short: Combined boundary and topographic survey for design of additions, pools, and major landscaping.
services.description_long: For projects that require both legal boundary information and detailed elevations, this combined service offers efficient data collection and a single deliverable supporting engineers, architects, and landscape designers. Ideal for pool installations, significant additions, and complex grading improvements on residential lots up to 2 acres.

services.name: Commercial Site Civil Design
services.description_short: Full-site civil engineering for commercial and mixed-use projects, from concept through construction.
services.description_long: Our commercial site civil design services cover due diligence, conceptual layouts, grading, drainage, utilities, paving, and construction administration. We coordinate closely with architects, landscape architects, and MEPs to create integrated solutions that meet municipal requirements and construction budgets.

services.name: Municipal Roadway & Intersection Design
services.description_short: Planning and design services for city and county roadway improvements, intersections, and sidewalks.
services.description_long: We provide complete roadway engineering services including corridor studies, preliminary engineering, final PS&E packages, and bid-phase support. Our experience includes urban arterials, rural highways, and multi-modal complete-streets projects throughout Texas.

services.name: Residential Drainage & Grading Consultation
services.description_short: Engineer review of existing drainage issues with conceptual grading recommendations for single-family homes.
services.description_long: An on-site or virtual consultation focused on diagnosing drainage problems such as ponding, erosion, and water intrusion around homes. We review available surveys, visit the site when needed, and prepare a short summary of recommended grading or drainage improvements. Detailed construction plans can be provided as a follow-up service.

services.name: Industrial Site Boundary & Topographic Survey
services.description_short: Combined boundary and topographic survey services for industrial and logistics facilities.
services.description_long: We support industrial clients with surveys covering large, complex sites that may include rail spurs, extensive paving, and structured parking. Deliverables are tailored for both site civil and structural design teams.

services.name: Construction Staking
services.description_short: Construction layout and staking services for buildings, paving, and utilities.
services.description_long: Our field crews provide construction staking for a wide range of projects, from small building pads to large subdivisions and tilt-wall warehouses. We work from engineer-issued CAD files and coordinate with contractors to optimize field efficiency.

service_regions.name: Greater Austin Area
service_regions.description: Primary service region covering Austin and surrounding communities, including Round Rock, Cedar Park, Georgetown, Pflugerville, Leander, and Buda. Most residential surveying and civil engineering services, including boundary surveys for lots under 1 acre, are available throughout this area.

service_regions.name: Dallas–Fort Worth Metroplex
service_regions.description: North Texas service region including Dallas, Fort Worth, Plano, Frisco, Arlington, and surrounding cities. Focus areas include commercial surveying, transportation design, and land development engineering.

service_regions.name: San Antonio Region
service_regions.description: Covers San Antonio, New Braunfels, Schertz, and nearby communities. Services include municipal roadway design, commercial site development, and supporting surveys.

service_regions.name: Houston Metro Area
service_regions.description: Selective services for industrial and commercial clients in the Houston metropolitan area, including boundary, topographic, and ALTA/NSPS land title surveys.

portfolio_projects.title: Round Rock Arterial Widening & Intersection Improvements
portfolio_projects.summary: Design and survey support for widening a key arterial roadway in Round Rock, including intersection upgrades and new turn lanes.
portfolio_projects.description: This multi-phase project widened approximately 2.3 miles of a four-lane arterial to six lanes with raised medians, added dual left-turn lanes at two signalized intersections, and upgraded pedestrian crossings to current ADA standards. Our team provided corridor surveying, roadway and drainage design, traffic control phasing, and construction staking. The design balanced limited right-of-way, heavy peak-hour traffic, and adjacent commercial driveways while improving safety and capacity.

portfolio_projects.title: Austin Complete Street Corridor Upgrade
portfolio_projects.summary: Conversion of a suburban four-lane roadway into a complete street with bike lanes, medians, and improved crossings.
portfolio_projects.description: The project reconstructed 1.8 miles of an aging corridor to incorporate protected bike lanes, wider sidewalks, landscaped medians, and upgraded drainage infrastructure. Our role included preliminary engineering alternatives, public meeting exhibits, horizontal and vertical roadway design, storm drain sizing, and coordination of utility relocations. Field survey and traffic data collection supported design decisions, and we provided ongoing response to contractor RFIs during construction.

portfolio_projects.title: Dallas Signalized Intersection Reconstruction
portfolio_projects.summary: Reconstruction of a congested urban intersection in Dallas to improve capacity and safety.
portfolio_projects.description: For this urban intersection, our team reconfigured lane assignments, introduced new turn lanes, and optimized pedestrian crossings. Detailed topographic and utility surveys informed design to minimize conflicts with existing underground infrastructure. We prepared paving, drainage, signing and pavement marking, and traffic signal plans in coordination with the Citys signal group.

portfolio_projects.title: San Antonio Corridor Operations & Safety Improvements
portfolio_projects.summary: Operational and safety upgrades along a 3-mile arterial in San Antonio, including access management and intersection enhancements.
portfolio_projects.description: Working with San Antonio Public Works, we identified low-cost operational changes to reduce crashes and improve travel time reliability along a congested arterial. The final design included new median openings, consolidated driveways, extended turn bays, and targeted signal timing and phasing changes. Roadway geometrics were refined using updated traffic counts and turning-movement data.

portfolio_projects.title: Austin Bus Rapid Transit Corridor Feasibility Study
portfolio_projects.summary: Transportation planning and preliminary design support for a proposed BRT corridor in Austin.
portfolio_projects.description: Our team evaluated multiple alignment and station-location options for a proposed Bus Rapid Transit corridor. Work included conceptual roadway cross-sections, queue jump and transit signal priority concepts, and high-level cost estimates. Survey control and limited topographic data were collected at key constrained locations to validate design assumptions.

portfolio_projects.title: Houston Port Access Roadway Improvements
portfolio_projects.summary: Geometric and drainage upgrades to improve truck access to an industrial terminal near the Houston Ship Channel.
portfolio_projects.description: Design work focused on improving heavy-truck circulation, adding dedicated turn lanes, and upgrading pavement sections to handle higher axle loads. Comprehensive drainage modeling addressed frequent ponding issues near terminal gates.

portfolio_projects.title: Hillside Residential Site Design  Austin
portfolio_projects.summary: Civil site design and drainage planning for a custom home on a steep hillside lot in Austin.
portfolio_projects.description: The project included grading, driveway alignment, and drainage design for a challenging hillside lot in west Austin. A combined boundary and topographic survey informed retaining wall layouts and building placement to minimize cut and fill.

portfolio_projects.title: Round Rock Residential Drainage Improvements
portfolio_projects.summary: Engineered grading and drainage solution for a neighborhood with recurring yard flooding in Round Rock.
portfolio_projects.description: Following multiple homeowner complaints, the HOA engaged our team to investigate drainage issues behind a row of homes. Field survey and spot elevations identified a blocked overland flow path. We designed swales, inlets, and a small storm drain system to redirect runoff to the public system.

portfolio_projects.title: Dallas Neighborhood Commercial Center
portfolio_projects.summary: Full civil site design and surveying for a 2-acre neighborhood commercial development in Dallas.
portfolio_projects.description: We provided boundary and topographic surveying, civil site design, and construction support for a small retail center including parking, utilities, and onsite stormwater controls.

portfolio_projects.title: Georgetown Residential Subdivision Platting & Survey
portfolio_projects.summary: Boundary, topographic, and platting services for a 45-lot single-family subdivision in Georgetown.
portfolio_projects.description: Our surveying team supported preliminary and final platting, followed by detailed construction staking for streets, utilities, and lots.

portfolio_projects.title: San Antonio Neighborhood Drainage Channel Improvements
portfolio_projects.summary: Surveying and design support for concrete channel repairs and bank stabilization in a residential area.
portfolio_projects.description: The project repaired failing concrete drainage channels and stabilized eroding banks adjacent to homes. High-resolution topographic survey data supported hydraulic modeling and design.

portfolio_projects.title: Dallas Industrial Park Boundary & Topographic Survey
portfolio_projects.summary: Large-scale boundary and topographic survey for a multi-building industrial park in Dallas.
portfolio_projects.description: Our crews surveyed over 60 acres of developed industrial property, including rail spurs, loading docks, and extensive utility networks, to support redevelopment planning.

portfolio_projects.title: Austin Residential Pool Site Plan & Survey
portfolio_projects.summary: Boundary/topographic survey and site plan for a backyard pool on a sub-1-acre lot in Austin.
portfolio_projects.description: This project paired a boundary and topographic survey with an engineer-prepared site plan showing the proposed pool, decking, and drainage patterns to satisfy City of Austin permitting requirements.
